Perfect. Now let me create a comprehensive summary document based on my analysis. Let me organize this thoroughly: ## Comprehensive Analysis: Cleaning Card Rendering & Data Flow Based on my thorough review of the code, here's the complete picture of how swap/takeover/done states flow through to rendering: ### 1. WEEK ROW DATA STRUCTURE (sensor.py, lines 646-676) Each week row in the `weeks` array contains these fields: ``` { "week_start": "YYYY-MM-DD", # ISO format Monday date "week_end": "YYYY-MM-DD", # ISO format Sunday date "week_number": int, # ISO week number # Assignee info "assignee_member_id": int, # Current effective assignee "assignee_name": str, # Display name of current assignee "assignee_user_id": str | null, # HA user ID of current assignee # Original/baseline (pre-override) "original_assignee_member_id": int, # Baseline assignee before override "original_assignee_name": str, "original_assignee_user_id": str | null, "baseline_assignee_member_id": int, # Same as original_assignee_member_id "baseline_assignee_name": str, # Override details "override_type": "manual_swap" | "compensation" | null, "override_source": "manual" | "takeover_completion" | null, "source_week_start": "YYYY-MM-DD" | null, # Week that triggered this override (for swap returns/compensation) # Status fields "status": "pending" | "done" | "missed", "completion_mode": "own" | "takeover" | null, "completed_by_member_id": int | null, "completed_by_name": str | null, "completed_at": "ISO datetime" | null, # UI metadata "note": "swap" | "swap return week" | "make-up shift" | "", "is_current": bool, "is_past": bool, "is_previous": bool, "is_next": bool, # History & notifications "history": [{ activity events }], # Last 7 days of activity "notification_slots": [{ slot data }], "notification_has_issue": bool # True if any slot state in {failed, skipped, suppressed, missing} } ``` ### 2. WEEK ROW TEMPLATE RENDERING (card.js, lines 1099-1214) **The critical rendering section:** #### Assignment display (lines 1198-1202): ```html
${isDone ? '' : ""} ${assigneeName} ${overrideBadge}
``` - When `isDone` (status === "done"), the text gets strikethrough styling - The check icon appears next to the assignee name - **Badge logic** (lines 1125-1133): - Shows "Swap" if `override_type === "manual_swap"` - Shows "Swap return" if `override_type === "compensation"` AND `override_source === "manual"` - Shows "Make-up" if `override_type === "compensation"` AND `override_source !== "manual"` (takeover completion) #### Secondary note/metadata (lines 1135-1145): ```javascript const secondary = row.override_type === "manual_swap" && originalLabel && originalLabel !== assigneeLabel ? `${this._escape(`Swapped with ${originalLabel}`)}` : row.override_type === "compensation" ? (() => { const compensationNote = this._compensationNote(row, assigneeLabel, originalLabel); return compensationNote ? `${this._escape(compensationNote)}` : ""; })() : ""; ``` #### Done-by metadata (lines 1103-1108): ```javascript let doneMeta = ""; if (isDone && completedByName) { doneMeta = `Done by ${completedByName}`; } else if (isMissed) { doneMeta = 'Not confirmed'; } ``` ### 3. COMPENSATION NOTE LOGIC (card.js, lines 287-299) ```javascript _compensationNote(row, assigneeLabel, originalLabel) { if (row?.override_type !== "compensation") { return ""; } if (row?.override_source === "manual") { // This is a swap return - shows when original person swaps back const swappedWith = originalLabel || "another flatmate"; const elapsed = this._weekDistanceLabel(row?.source_week_start, row?.week_start); return elapsed ? `Swapped with ${swappedWith} ${elapsed}` : `Swapped with ${swappedWith}`; } // Takeover completion compensation return `Make-up shift: ${assigneeLabel} covers ${originalLabel || "the original turn"}`; } ``` **Example outputs:** - Manual swap return: "Swapped with Alice 1 week ago" - Takeover compensation: "Make-up shift: Bob covers Alice's original turn" ### 4. STRIKETHROUGH CSS (card.js, lines 1963-1967) ```css .assignee.striked span { text-decoration: line-through; text-decoration-thickness: 1.5px; text-decoration-color: color-mix(in srgb, var(--success-color, #4caf50) 65%, currentColor); } ``` - Applied when `isDone === true` - Line color is 65% success-color (green) mixed with text color ### 5. HISTORY MODAL TEMPLATE (card.js, lines 1684-1716) ```html ${!compactMode && this._historyModalOpen && historyModalWeek ? `

Shift history

${this._escape(historyWeekLabel)}

Assigned flatmate: ${this._escape(historyAssigneeName)}

Notification delivery

    ${historySlotRows}

Recent events (last 7 days)

    ${historyEventRows}
` : ""} ``` #### Notification slots rendering (lines 1407-1428): Each slot shows: ```html
  • ${slot.label} ${slot.state_label}
    ${detail ? `${detail}` : ""} ${timeLabel ? `${timeLabel}` : ""}
    • ``` Slot states and meanings (from sensor.py, lines 214-224): - `"sent"` → "Sent" - `"test_redirected"` → "Sent (test mode)" - `"failed"` → "Failed" - `"skipped"` → "Skipped" - `"suppressed"` → "Suppressed" - `"scheduled"` → "Scheduled" - `"not_required"` → "Skipped — done early" - `"no_data"` → "No data" - `"missing"` → "Missing" #### History events rendering (lines 1429-1452): ```html
  • ${event.summary} ${when}
    ${action} ${reason ? `${reason}` : ""}
    • ``` ### 6. NOTIFICATION SLOTS DATA (sensor.py, lines 200-288) The `_notification_slots_for_week` function builds 3 notification slot definitions: ```python slot_defs = [ ("monday_11", "Monday assignment reminder", 0, 11), # Monday 11:00 AM ("sunday_18", "Sunday evening reminder", 6, 18), # Sunday 6:00 PM ("sunday_21", "Sunday final reminder", 6, 21), # Sunday 9:00 PM ] ``` For each slot: 1. Look for latest `cleaning_notification_dispatch` event in history for that slot 2. If found, use the dispatch status (sent, failed, etc.) 3. If not found: - If now < scheduled time → state = "scheduled" - If done early (before reminder time) → state = "not_required" - If other dispatches exist but not this one → state = "missing" - Otherwise → state = "no_data" Each slot returned has: ```python { "slot": "monday_11" | "sunday_18" | "sunday_21", "label": str, "state": str, # notification state "state_label": str, # human-readable state "detail": str, # description of state "last_event_at": "ISO datetime" | None, } ``` ### 7. CLEANING HISTORY DATA (sensor.py, lines 141-193) The `_build_cleaning_history_by_week` function: 1. Filters activity rows where `domain == "cleaning"` 2. Takes only events from last 7 days (cutoff = now - 7 days) 3. Groups by week_start (extracting from payload fields: `week_start`, `source_week_start`, `compensation_week_start`, `return_week_start`) 4. Sorts by created_at descending (newest first) 5. Keeps max 30 items per week 6. Each history item has: ```python { "id": int, "created_at": "ISO datetime", "summary": str, # Human-readable summary (e.g., "Alice marked this shift done") "action": str, # action from activity (e.g., "cleaning_done", "cleaning_takeover_done") "actor_name": str, "notification_slot": str | None, # If it's a notification dispatch "dispatch_status": str | None, # If it's a notification dispatch "reason": str | None, } ``` ### 8. BACKEND DATA FLOW: get_schedule() (cleaning.py, lines 930-965) This is what's called to build the schedule data: ```python def get_schedule(session: Session, *, weeks_ahead: int, from_week_start: date | None = None) -> list[dict]: start = from_week_start or week_start_for(now_utc()) rows: list[dict] = [] for offset in range(max(weeks_ahead, 0)): week = add_weeks(start, offset) assignment = ensure_assignment(session, week) baseline_id = baseline_assignee_member_id(session, week) effective_id, override = effective_assignee_member_id(session, week) source_week_start = None # If there's an override with source_event_id, extract source_week_start from event payload if override is not None and override.source_event_id is not None: event_id = int(override.source_event_id) if event_id not in source_week_by_event_id: event = session.get(ActivityEvent, event_id) source_week_by_event_id[event_id] = _parse_source_week_start( event.payload_json if event is not None else None ) source_week_start = source_week_by_event_id.get(event_id) rows.append( { "week_start": week, "baseline_assignee_member_id": baseline_id, "effective_assignee_member_id": effective_id, "override_type": override.type.value if override else None, # "manual_swap" | "compensation" | None "override_source": override.source.value if override else None, # "manual" | "takeover_completion" | None "source_week_start": source_week_start, "status": assignment.status.value, # "pending" | "done" | "missed" "completed_by_member_id": assignment.completed_by_member_id, "completion_mode": assignment.completion_mode, # "own" | "takeover" | None "completed_at": assignment.completed_at, } ) session.commit() return rows ``` ### 9. OVERRIDE TYPES IN DATABASE (cleaning.py) **Manual Swap** (lines 291-444): - `override_type = OverrideType.MANUAL_SWAP` - `override_source = OverrideSource.MANUAL` - Creates TWO overrides: 1. Swap week: member_from_id → member_to_id (assignee changes to member_to) 2. Return week (compensation): member_from_id ← member_to_id (assignee changes back) - Both linked via `source_event_id` pointing to the swap event **Takeover Completion** (lines 801-889): - Original week marked as done with `completion_mode = "takeover"` - Creates a COMPENSATION override at the cleaner's next regular week: - `override_type = OverrideType.COMPENSATION` - `override_source = OverrideSource.TAKEOVER_COMPLETION` - `member_from_id = cleaner_member_id` - `member_to_id = original_assignee_member_id` - `source_event_id` links to the takeover_done event ### 10. MODAL CSS (card.js, lines 2092-2320) **Modal backdrop:** ```css .modal-backdrop { position: fixed; inset: 0; background: rgba(0, 0, 0, 0.38); display: grid; place-items: center; z-index: 20; padding: 14px; box-sizing: border-box; } ``` **Modal container:** ```css .modal { width: min(560px, 100%); background: var(--card-background-color); border: 1px solid var(--divider-color); border-radius: 14px; box-shadow: 0 12px 36px rgba(0, 0, 0, 0.3); padding: 14px; display: grid; gap: 10px; } .history-modal { /* Same as .modal - no additional styling */ } ``` **History slot styling:** ```css .history-slot-row, .history-event-row { border: 1px solid var(--divider-color); border-radius: 10px; padding: 8px 10px; display: grid; gap: 4px; background: var(--card-background-color); } /* Slot state badges */ .slot-state.sent, .slot-state.test_redirected, .slot-state.not_required { color: var(--success-color, #4caf50); border-color: color-mix(in srgb, var(--success-color, #4caf50) 45%, var(--divider-color)); } .slot-state.failed, .slot-state.skipped, .slot-state.suppressed, .slot-state.missing { color: var(--warning-color, #f57c00); border-color: color-mix(in srgb, var(--warning-color, #f57c00) 45%, var(--divider-color)); } ``` --- ## Summary The entire flow works as follows: 1. **Backend** (`cleaning.py`: `get_schedule()`) builds schedule rows with `override_type`, `override_source`, `source_week_start`, and completion info 2. **Sensor** (`sensor.py`: `CleaningScheduleSensor`) enriches rows with names, history, and notification slots 3. **Card template** (card.js: `_render()`) renders week rows showing: - Assignee name (strikethrough if done) - Badge showing override type (Swap, Make-up, Swap return) - Secondary note explaining the swap/compensation - "Done by X" if completed by someone other than assignee 4. **History modal** (card.js) displays notification delivery status and activity events from the last 7 days The key insight: **`assignee_member_id` vs `original_assignee_member_id`** determines the entire visual display. When they differ, there's an override active. The `override_type` and `override_source` fields tell you *what kind* of override and *why* it was created.

    • ``` Slot states and meanings (from sensor.py, lines 214-224): - `"sent"` → "Sent" - `"test_redirected"` → "Sent (test mode)" - `"failed"` → "Failed" - `"skipped"` → "Skipped" - `"suppressed"` → "Suppressed" - `"scheduled"` → "Scheduled" - `"not_required"` → "Skipped — done early" - `"no_data"` → "No data" - `"missing"` → "Missing" #### History events rendering (lines 1429-1452): ```html
  • ${event.summary} ${when}
    ${action} ${reason ? `${reason}` : ""}
    • ``` ### 6. NOTIFICATION SLOTS DATA (sensor.py, lines 200-288) The `_notification_slots_for_week` function builds 3 notification slot definitions: ```python slot_defs = [ ("monday_11", "Monday assignment reminder", 0, 11), # Monday 11:00 AM ("sunday_18", "Sunday evening reminder", 6, 18), # Sunday 6:00 PM ("sunday_21", "Sunday final reminder", 6, 21), # Sunday 9:00 PM ] ``` For each slot: 1. Look for latest `cleaning_notification_dispatch` event in history for that slot 2. If found, use the dispatch status (sent, failed, etc.) 3. If not found: - If now < scheduled time → state = "scheduled" - If done early (before reminder time) → state = "not_required" - If other dispatches exist but not this one → state = "missing" - Otherwise → state = "no_data" Each slot returned has: ```python { "slot": "monday_11" | "sunday_18" | "sunday_21", "label": str, "state": str, # notification state "state_label": str, # human-readable state "detail": str, # description of state "last_event_at": "ISO datetime" | None, } ``` ### 7. CLEANING HISTORY DATA (sensor.py, lines 141-193) The `_build_cleaning_history_by_week` function: 1. Filters activity rows where `domain == "cleaning"` 2. Takes only events from last 7 days (cutoff = now - 7 days) 3. Groups by week_start (extracting from payload fields: `week_start`, `source_week_start`, `compensation_week_start`, `return_week_start`) 4. Sorts by created_at descending (newest first) 5. Keeps max 30 items per week 6. Each history item has: ```python { "id": int, "created_at": "ISO datetime", "summary": str, # Human-readable summary (e.g., "Alice marked this shift done") "action": str, # action from activity (e.g., "cleaning_done", "cleaning_takeover_done") "actor_name": str, "notification_slot": str | None, # If it's a notification dispatch "dispatch_status": str | None, # If it's a notification dispatch "reason": str | None, } ``` ### 8. BACKEND DATA FLOW: get_schedule() (cleaning.py, lines 930-965) This is what's called to build the schedule data: ```python def get_schedule(session: Session, *, weeks_ahead: int, from_week_start: date | None = None) -> list[dict]: start = from_week_start or week_start_for(now_utc()) rows: list[dict] = [] for offset in range(max(weeks_ahead, 0)): week = add_weeks(start, offset) assignment = ensure_assignment(session, week) baseline_id = baseline_assignee_member_id(session, week) effective_id, override = effective_assignee_member_id(session, week) source_week_start = None # If there's an override with source_event_id, extract source_week_start from event payload if override is not None and override.source_event_id is not None: event_id = int(override.source_event_id) if event_id not in source_week_by_event_id: event = session.get(ActivityEvent, event_id) source_week_by_event_id[event_id] = _parse_source_week_start( event.payload_json if event is not None else None ) source_week_start = source_week_by_event_id.get(event_id) rows.append( { "week_start": week, "baseline_assignee_member_id": baseline_id, "effective_assignee_member_id": effective_id, "override_type": override.type.value if override else None, # "manual_swap" | "compensation" | None "override_source": override.source.value if override else None, # "manual" | "takeover_completion" | None "source_week_start": source_week_start, "status": assignment.status.value, # "pending" | "done" | "missed" "completed_by_member_id": assignment.completed_by_member_id, "completion_mode": assignment.completion_mode, # "own" | "takeover" | None "completed_at": assignment.completed_at, } ) session.commit() return rows ``` ### 9. OVERRIDE TYPES IN DATABASE (cleaning.py) **Manual Swap** (lines 291-444): - `override_type = OverrideType.MANUAL_SWAP` - `override_source = OverrideSource.MANUAL` - Creates TWO overrides: 1. Swap week: member_from_id → member_to_id (assignee changes to member_to) 2. Return week (compensation): member_from_id ← member_to_id (assignee changes back) - Both linked via `source_event_id` pointing to the swap event **Takeover Completion** (lines 801-889): - Original week marked as done with `completion_mode = "takeover"` - Creates a COMPENSATION override at the cleaner's next regular week: - `override_type = OverrideType.COMPENSATION` - `override_source = OverrideSource.TAKEOVER_COMPLETION` - `member_from_id = cleaner_member_id` - `member_to_id = original_assignee_member_id` - `source_event_id` links to the takeover_done event ### 10. MODAL CSS (card.js, lines 2092-2320) **Modal backdrop:** ```css .modal-backdrop { position: fixed; inset: 0; background: rgba(0, 0, 0, 0.38); display: grid; place-items: center; z-index: 20; padding: 14px; box-sizing: border-box; } ``` **Modal container:** ```css .modal { width: min(560px, 100%); background: var(--card-background-color); border: 1px solid var(--divider-color); border-radius: 14px; box-shadow: 0 12px 36px rgba(0, 0, 0, 0.3); padding: 14px; display: grid; gap: 10px; } .history-modal { /* Same as .modal - no additional styling */ } ``` **History slot styling:** ```css .history-slot-row, .history-event-row { border: 1px solid var(--divider-color); border-radius: 10px; padding: 8px 10px; display: grid; gap: 4px; background: var(--card-background-color); } /* Slot state badges */ .slot-state.sent, .slot-state.test_redirected, .slot-state.not_required { color: var(--success-color, #4caf50); border-color: color-mix(in srgb, var(--success-color, #4caf50) 45%, var(--divider-color)); } .slot-state.failed, .slot-state.skipped, .slot-state.suppressed, .slot-state.missing { color: var(--warning-color, #f57c00); border-color: color-mix(in srgb, var(--warning-color, #f57c00) 45%, var(--divider-color)); } ``` --- ## Summary The entire flow works as follows: 1. **Backend** (`cleaning.py`: `get_schedule()`) builds schedule rows with `override_type`, `override_source`, `source_week_start`, and completion info 2. **Sensor** (`sensor.py`: `CleaningScheduleSensor`) enriches rows with names, history, and notification slots 3. **Card template** (card.js: `_render()`) renders week rows showing: - Assignee name (strikethrough if done) - Badge showing override type (Swap, Make-up, Swap return) - Secondary note explaining the swap/compensation - "Done by X" if completed by someone other than assignee 4. **History modal** (card.js) displays notification delivery status and activity events from the last 7 days The key insight: **`assignee_member_id` vs `original_assignee_member_id`** determines the entire visual display. When they differ, there's an override active. The `override_type` and `override_source` fields tell you *what kind* of override and *why* it was created.
Now I have a complete picture. Let me read a few specific sections to nail down the week row swap display issue.

content.text:
Now I understand the problem clearly. When there's a swap and the swapped-in person marks it done:
- `assignee_member_id` = swapped-in person (the one who actually cleaned)
- `completed_by_member_id` = same person (they marked their own swap shift done)
- The card shows: ~~SwappedPerson~~ (strikethrough) with "Done by SwappedPerson"
- But it should show: SwappedPerson ~~done~~ with context "Originally OriginalPerson's shift, swapped"

Let me now write the plan.

input.content:
# Plan: Redesign cleaning shift detail modal and fix swap display ## Context Three issues to address: 1. **Week row swap display is confusing** — When a swap happens and the swapped-in person marks it done, the card shows their name struck through with "Done by {same person}". Should instead show the person who did it clearly, with context about the swap. 2. **History modal needs to become a shift detail view** — Currently shows two separate sections (notification slots + recent events). Needs to be a comprehensive shift detail view explaining the full context (status, swap details, takeover info) with a unified timeline merging notifications and events, using icons, newest-first ordering, with future scheduled notifications shown at top. 3. **Modal not scrollable** — Long content overflows instead of scrolling. ## Files to modify - `custom_components/hass_flatmate/frontend/hass-flatmate-cleaning-card.js` — Week row template + modal template + CSS - `custom_components/hass_flatmate/sensor.py` — Enrich week data with timeline entries merging notifications + events ## Changes ### 1. Fix week row display for swaps/takeovers (card.js) **Current behavior (wrong):** - Shows: ~~Martin~~ [Swap badge] / "Swapped with María" / "Done by Martin" - The assignee name (Martin, the swapped-in person) gets struck through, then "Done by Martin" is redundant **New behavior:** - When `completion_mode === "own"` and `override_type === "manual_swap"`: - Show: ✓ Martin [Swap badge] (with strikethrough on name, green check) - Below: "Originally María's shift" - No "Done by" line (redundant — assignee did it themselves) - When `completion_mode === "takeover"` (someone else did the shift): - Show: ~~AssigneeName~~ (strikethrough) - Below: "Taken over by {completed_by_name}" - When `completion_mode === "own"` and no override: - Show: ✓ AssigneeName (strikethrough, green check) - No "Done by" (they did their own shift, obvious) - Only show "Done by {name}" when `completed_by_member_id !== assignee_member_id` (actual different person doing it) **Summary of `secondary` note changes:** - `manual_swap`: "Originally {originalName}'s shift" (instead of "Swapped with {originalName}") - `compensation` with `override_source === "manual"`: "Swap return — {originalName}'s regular week" with relative timing - `compensation` with takeover: "Make-up for {originalName}" (shorter) ### 2. Make modal scrollable (card.js CSS) Add to `.history-modal`: ```css max-height: min(85vh, 680px); overflow-y: auto; ``` ### 3. Redesign modal as shift detail view (card.js + sensor.py) #### 3a. Shift context section (top of modal, replaces current simple "Assigned flatmate: X") Show a rich context block explaining the shift: - **Header**: "Shift details" (rename from "Shift history") - **Week label**: "This week (Feb 10 – 16)" — already exists - **Status pill**: Done / Pending / Missed — reuse existing badge styling - **Assignee**: "{Name}" with clear label "Assigned to" - **If swap**: Explain "This shift was swapped with {otherPerson}. {otherPerson}'s regular week is {dateRange} ({relativeLabel})." - **If swap return (compensation/manual)**: "This is a swap return week. Originally {person}'s turn, swapped with {assigneeName} {relativeLabel}." - **If takeover compensation**: "This is a make-up shift. {assigneeName} is covering for {originalName} after a takeover on {sourceWeekRange}." - **If done**: "Completed by {name} on {date}" or "Completed on {date}" if same as assignee - **If takeover done**: "{completedByName} took over this shift from {assigneeName}" - **If missed**: "This shift was not confirmed as done" #### 3b. Unified timeline (replaces two separate sections) **In sensor.py**, build a `timeline` list per week that merges: - Notification slots (both dispatched and scheduled future ones) - Activity events (cleaning_done, cleaning_undone, swap events, etc.) Each timeline entry has: ```python { "type": "notification" | "event", "timestamp": "ISO datetime" | None, # actual time or scheduled time "is_future": bool, "icon": "mdi:bell-outline" | "mdi:check" | "mdi:swap-horizontal" | "mdi:undo" | ..., "summary": str, "detail": str | None, "state": str | None, # for notifications: sent/scheduled/etc. "state_label": str | None, } ``` **Sorting**: Future items first (sorted by scheduled time ascending), then past items sorted by timestamp descending (newest first). This means you see upcoming notifications at top, then scroll down through history. **Timeline entry icons** (by type): - `cleaning_notification_dispatch` (sent): `mdi:bell-check` - `cleaning_notification_dispatch` (failed/skipped/suppressed): `mdi:bell-off` - Scheduled notification (future): `mdi:bell-clock` - `cleaning_done`: `mdi:check-circle` - `cleaning_undone`: `mdi:undo-variant` - `cleaning_takeover_done`: `mdi:account-switch` - `cleaning_swap_created`: `mdi:swap-horizontal` - `cleaning_swap_updated`: `mdi:swap-horizontal` - `cleaning_swap_canceled`: `mdi:close-circle` - `cleaning_compensation_planned`: `mdi:calendar-plus` - `cleaning_override_auto_canceled_member_inactive`: `mdi:account-remove` - Scheduled notification (not_required): `mdi:bell-minus` - Default: `mdi:information` #### 3c. Timeline rendering in card (card.js) Each timeline entry rendered as: ```html
  • ${summary} ${state_badge_if_notification}
    ${detail ? `${detail}` : ""} ${timestamp ? `${formatted_time}` : ""}
    • ``` Visual: vertical timeline line on the left connecting the icon dots, future entries have a subtle dashed border or lighter styling to distinguish from past. #### 3d. Remove `notification_slots` separate section The notification slots data is now merged into the unified timeline. Remove the `notification_slots` key from sensor data and the separate "Notification delivery" panel from the modal. **Actually, keep `notification_slots` in sensor data** — it's still used by the inline `notificationMeta` on week rows. But the modal no longer renders them as a separate section. ### 4. Sensor changes (sensor.py) Add new function `_build_week_timeline()` that: 1. Takes the week's `history` list and `notification_slots` list 2. Converts each history event into a timeline entry with icon 3. Converts each notification slot into a timeline entry with icon 4. Filters out notification dispatch events from history (they're already represented by notification slots to avoid duplicates) 5. Merges and sorts: future items first (ascending by time), then past items (descending by time) 6. Returns the `timeline` list Add `timeline` to each week row dict in `CleaningScheduleSensor.extra_state_attributes`. ### 5. Keep notification_has_issue for inline week row display The `notification_has_issue` flag and inline `notificationMeta` on week rows stay as-is — they give a quick at-a-glance indicator without opening the modal. ## Verification 1. `node --check custom_components/hass_flatmate/frontend/hass-flatmate-cleaning-card.js` — JS syntax valid 2. `python3 -m compileall custom_components/hass_flatmate` — integration compiles 3. `cd addon/hass_flatmate_service && pytest` — backend tests pass (no backend changes)

This means you see upcoming notifications at top, then scroll down through history. **Timeline entry icons** (by type): - `cleaning_notification_dispatch` (sent): `mdi:bell-check` - `cleaning_notification_dispatch` (failed/skipped/suppressed): `mdi:bell-off` - Scheduled notification (future): `mdi:bell-clock` - `cleaning_done`: `mdi:check-circle` - `cleaning_undone`: `mdi:undo-variant` - `cleaning_takeover_done`: `mdi:account-switch` - `cleaning_swap_created`: `mdi:swap-horizontal` - `cleaning_swap_updated`: `mdi:swap-horizontal` - `cleaning_swap_canceled`: `mdi:close-circle` - `cleaning_compensation_planned`: `mdi:calendar-plus` - `cleaning_override_auto_canceled_member_inactive`: `mdi:account-remove` - Scheduled notification (not_required): `mdi:bell-minus` - Default: `mdi:information` #### 3c. Timeline rendering in card (card.js) Each timeline entry rendered as: ```html
  • ${summary} ${state_badge_if_notification}
    ${detail ? `${detail}` : ""} ${timestamp ? `${formatted_time}` : ""}
    • ``` Visual: vertical timeline line on the left connecting the icon dots, future entries have a subtle dashed border or lighter styling to distinguish from past. #### 3d. Remove `notification_slots` separate section The notification slots data is now merged into the unified timeline. Remove the `notification_slots` key from sensor data and the separate "Notification delivery" panel from the modal. **Actually, keep `notification_slots` in sensor data** — it's still used by the inline `notificationMeta` on week rows. But the modal no longer renders them as a separate section. ### 4. Sensor changes (sensor.py) Add new function `_build_week_timeline()` that: 1. Takes the week's `history` list and `notification_slots` list 2. Converts each history event into a timeline entry with icon 3. Converts each notification slot into a timeline entry with icon 4. Filters out notification dispatch events from history (they're already represented by notification slots to avoid duplicates) 5. Merges and sorts: future items first (ascending by time), then past items (descending by time) 6. Returns the `timeline` list Add `timeline` to each week row dict in `CleaningScheduleSensor.extra_state_attributes`. ### 5. Keep notification_has_issue for inline week row display The `notification_has_issue` flag and inline `notificationMeta` on week rows stay as-is — they give a quick at-a-glance indicator without opening the modal. ## Verification 1. `node --check custom_components/hass_flatmate/frontend/hass-flatmate-cleaning-card.js` — JS syntax valid 2. `python3 -m compileall custom_components/hass_flatmate` — integration compiles 3. `cd addon/hass_flatmate_service && pytest` — backend tests pass (no backend changes)

# Plan: Improve notification slot UX in shift history modal

## Context

The shift history modal (click any week row) shows notification delivery status per slot, but the current implementation has several UX gaps:

- **"Not required"** is vague — should explain *why* (e.g. cleaning was already marked done before the reminder was due)
- **"Pending"** says nothing about when it'll fire — should show scheduled time
- **"Missing"** is alarming — could mean a bug or the feature wasn't deployed yet. Needs a human-friendly explanation
- **No detail text** for inferred states (pending/missing/not_required) — only actual dispatch events get explanatory detail
- Slot labels like "Mon 11:00 assignment" are technical jargon
- **"Not required"** looks identical to "Pending" visually — should look resolved/positive since it means "all good"
- Future weeks where all slots are "pending" show no "Notification issue" but also no positive indicator — that's fine, but the modal should still be clear
- Past weeks before this feature existed show all "missing" which is misleading

## Changes

### 1. Sensor: richer slot metadata (`sensor.py` — `_notification_slots_for_week`)

Add a `detail` string to every inferred (non-dispatch) slot:

| State | Current label | New label | Detail text |
|-------|--------------|-----------|-------------|
| `pending` | "Pending" | "Scheduled" | "Scheduled for Mon, Feb 16 at 11:00" (formatted slot_moment) |
| `not_required` | "Not required" | "Skipped — already done" | "Cleaning was marked done before this reminder was due" |
| `missing` (monday slot, past week) | "Missing" | "No data" | "Notification tracking was not yet active for this week" |
| `missing` (sunday slot, completed_at is None, past) | "Missing" | "No data" | "Notification tracking was not yet active for this week" |

Update `status_labels` dict and add `detail` to all inferred slots.

Also: for `not_required`, also cover the case where `status == "done"` on the row but `completed_at` is `None` (old data before completed_at was tracked). Add the `status` parameter to the function so it can detect this.

### 2. Sensor: pass week status into slot function (`sensor.py` — call site)

Pass `status=status_value` to `_notification_slots_for_week` so it can use the done/missed status for smarter inference.

For **missed** weeks: Sunday reminders that went out but the shift was never confirmed should show context about that too.

### 3. Card: human-friendly slot labels (`hass-flatmate-cleaning-card.js`)

The slot labels from the sensor are already the display labels. The sensor change handles this.

### 4. Card: add detail text for all slot states (`hass-flatmate-cleaning-card.js`)

The card already renders `detail` when present — no card template changes needed since we're now providing `detail` from the sensor for all states.

### 5. Card: distinct visual style for "Skipped — already done" (`hass-flatmate-cleaning-card.js`)

Add CSS for `.slot-state.not_required` to use success color (green) instead of secondary text color, since it represents a positive outcome (cleaning done early).

### 6. Card: "Notification issue" inline label should not trigger for `missing` on old weeks

Currently `notification_has_issue` in `sensor.py` flags `missing` as an issue. But for weeks before the feature existed, every slot is "missing" — this creates false warnings on every past week.

Fix: only flag `missing` as an issue if `now_local` is past the slot moment AND the week has at least one dispatch event (meaning the feature was active). Otherwise treat `missing` as informational ("no data") not an issue.

Better approach: rename the state itself. Use `"no_data"` for old weeks where we simply have no tracking, and keep `"missing"` only for weeks where we expected a dispatch but didn't get one (i.e., at least one other slot has dispatch data, or the week is current/recent enough that the feature should have been active).

Simplest heuristic: if a week has zero dispatch events in its history AND the slot moment is past → use `"no_data"` state. If a week has at least one dispatch event but this specific slot has none and its moment is past → use `"missing"`.

### 7. Summary of states after changes

| State | Badge label | Badge color | Detail | When |
|-------|------------|-------------|--------|------|
| `sent` | Sent | Green | From dispatch event summary | Dispatch recorded as sent |
| `test_redirected` | Sent (test) | Green | From dispatch event summary | Dispatch recorded in test mode |
| `failed` | Failed | Warning/orange | From dispatch event summary | Dispatch recorded as failed |
| `skipped` | Skipped | Warning/orange | From dispatch event summary | Dispatch skipped (bad service etc.) |
| `suppressed` | Suppressed | Warning/orange | From dispatch event summary | Test mode on, no target configured |
| `scheduled` | Scheduled | Secondary/neutral | "Scheduled for Mon, Feb 16 at 11:00" | Slot time is in the future |
| `not_required` | Skipped — done early | Green | "Cleaning was completed before this reminder was due" | Sunday slot, completed_at < slot time |
| `no_data` | No data | Secondary/neutral | "Notification tracking was not available for this week" | Past slot, no dispatch events for entire week |
| `missing` | Missing | Warning/orange | "Expected notification was not recorded" | Past slot, other slots have dispatch data but this one doesn't |

## Files to modify

1. **`custom_components/hass_flatmate/sensor.py`** — `_notification_slots_for_week()` function and its call site
2. **`custom_components/hass_flatmate/frontend/hass-flatmate-cleaning-card.js`** — CSS for `.slot-state.not_required`, `.slot-state.no_data`, `.slot-state.scheduled`

## Verification

1. `cd addon/hass_flatmate_service && pytest` — all 29 tests pass (no backend changes)
2. `node --check custom_components/hass_flatmate/frontend/hass-flatmate-cleaning-card.js` — JS syntax valid
3. `python -m compileall custom_components/hass_flatmate` — integration compiles
